Assembly: The assembly nxCaeSpeh1.1 is based on PacBio HiFi and Hi-C data derived from pools of individuals generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute's 959 Nematode Genomes project. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi.

Definition: Sitta europaea genome assembly, chromosome: 9
Description: Sitta europaea genome assembly, chromosome: 9
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Sittidae; Sitta
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly bSitEur1.1 of the Eurasian nuthatch, Sitta europaea. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSitEur is based on 34x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bSitEur1.1 of the Eurasian nuthatch, Sitta europaea. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSitEur is based on 34x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.
